title: skopeo copy doesn't fail if --encryption-key is ignored
body:
```
# skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.1.0
```

When copying a Docker image to a Docker Archive, the `--encryption-key` option is ignored, and instead the unencrypted image is saved:

This is because Docker archives are saved as schema 2 manifest, and not OCI with encrypted layer support.

```
root@65f8c6547c03:/# skopeo copy --encryption-key jwe:./publickey.pem docker://docker.io/library/nginx:1.17.8 docker-archive://test.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 68ced04f60ab done  
WARN[0006] Uncompressed digest for blob sha256:68ced04f60ab5c7a5f1d0b0b4e7572c5a4c8cce44866513d30d9df1a15277d6b previously recorded as sha256:f2cb0ecef392f2a630fa1205b874ab2e2aedf96de04d0b8838e4e728e28142da, now sha256:8191de27094c89bab360d57bdca35c0f6bfe50e5fc1028b906062c351754c404 
WARN[0006] Uncompressed digest for blob sha256:68ced04f60ab5c7a5f1d0b0b4e7572c5a4c8cce44866513d30d9df1a15277d6b previously recorded as sha256:8191de27094c89bab360d57bdca35c0f6bfe50e5fc1028b906062c351754c4Copying blob 68ced04f60ab done  
Copying blob c4039fd85dcc done  
WARN[0010] Uncompressed digest for blob sha256:c4039fd85dccc8e267c98447f8f1b27a402dbb4259d86586f4097acb5e6634af previously recorded as sha256:fe08d5d042ab93bee05f9cda17f1c57066e146b0704be2ff755d14c25e6aa5e8, now sha256:896d87efd59746ef1f97939a2306dcf9ce8747ca447f9df7003730c33752a369 
WARN[0010] Uncompressed digest for blob sha256:c4039fd85dccc8e267c98447f8f1b27a402dbb4259d86586f4097acCopying blob 68ced04f60ab done  
Copying blob c4039fd85dcc done  
Copying blob c16ce02d3d61 done  
WARN[0011] Uncompressed digest for blob sha256:c16ce02d3d6132f7059bf7e9ff6205cbf43e86c538ef981c37598afd27d01efa previously recorded as sha256:318be7aea8fc62d5910cca0d49311fa8d95502c90e2a91b7a4d78032a670b644, now sha256:77f548253a09e0587826e769cad0b6cb5fab2c76effa16360bfb7ab6e711994d 
WARN[0011] Uncompressed digest for blob sha256:c16ce02d3d6132f7059bf7e9ff6205cbf43e86c538ef981c37598afd27d01efa previously recorded as sha256:77f548253a09e0587826e769cad0b6cb5fab2c76effa16360bfb7ab6e711994d, now sha256:318be7aea8fc62d5910cca0d49311fa8d95502c90e2a91b7a4d78032a670b644 
Copying config a1523e8593 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

Above command succeeds, but is not encrypting the layers. The tar-file can be loaded directly into Docker and run.

```
$ docker load -i test.tar
Loaded image ID: sha256:a1523e859360df9ffe2b31a8270f5e16422609fe138c1636383efdc34b9ea2d6

$ docker run --rm -it --entrypoint=/bin/bash sha256:a1523e859360df9ffe2b31a8270f5e16422609fe138c1636383efdc34b9ea2d6

root@19df5a673ef3:/# ls
bin   dev  home  lib64	mnt  proc  run	 srv  tmp  var
boot  etc  lib	 media	opt  root  sbin  sys  usr
```

If we instead first save the encrypted image to oci, and _then_ saves the Docker tarball, it will fail, because the format is not supported:

```
root@65f8c6547c03:/# skopeo copy --encryption-key jwe:./publickey.pem docker://docker.io/library/nginx:1.17.8 oci://encrypted_nginx:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 68ced04f60ab done  
Copying blob c4039fd85dcc done  
Copying blob c16ce02d3d61 done  
Copying config 82cc4ad146 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

root@65f8c6547c03:/# skopeo copy oci://encrypted_nginx:latest docker-archive://test_from_oci.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 7ad1514e4f72 done  
Copying blob c33bb0a5c06c done  
Copying blob e891716107c0 [======================================] 203.0b / 203.0b
FATA[0000] Error creating an updated image manifest: Unknown media type during manifest conversion: "application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip+encrypted"
```

Here with the forced OCI format it fails as well:
```
skopeo copy --format=oci oci://encrypted_nginx:latest docker-archive://test_from_oci2.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 7ad1514e4f72 done  
Copying blob c33bb0a5c06c done  
Copying blob e891716107c0 done  
Copying config 82cc4ad146 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
FATA[0000] Error writing manifest: Unsupported manifest type, need a Docker schema 2 manifest
```

comments.body: @QiWang19 can you take a look at this please?

@mtrmac Just tried loading the tar into a clean dind, and it correctly fails importing the tar file with `Error processing tar file(exit status 1): archive/tar: invalid tar header`. So far so good.

And I can't extract the tarballs either.

If I try decrypting it with skopeo, it also fails, so there is an issue there (probably because the config is not made correctly).

```
skopeo copy --decryption-key ./key.pem docker-archive://test.tar docker-archive://test_unencrypted.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f2cb0ecef392 [======================================] 69.1MiB / 69.1MiB
Copying blob fe08d5d042ab [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 55.4MiB
Copying blob 318be7aea8fc [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 3.5KiB
FATA[0000] Error writing blob: Digest did not match, expected sha256:f2cb0ecef392f2a630fa1205b874ab2e2aedf96de04d0b8838e4e728e28142da, got sha256:f2af5aa68d4a7a84cdbf142a93cacd833ebb63db6b87ec5f6ad41604ca831309 
```
Thanks for confirmation. Yes, the `docker-archive` format can’t represent the encryption metadata, so it’s not surprising that it can’t be decrypted.
I take it that this is not an issue, reopen if I am mistaken.

It’s not OK to silently “succeed” in creating a non-decryptable encrypted image.

Transferring to c/image, though.
… actually, we only strictly need https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1002 to track this, not a separate issue. So, closing again.
containers/image#1002 is a PR, not an issue, will you transfer this issue to c/image?
De-facto @rhatdan is setting the policies as to what is kept open/closed, so I’ll leave it up to him.

@mtrmac No problem keeping issues open, if they are going to be worked on and fixed.  Just leaving them rotting, forever, I have a problem with.  If this issue is going to be fixed in containers/image then the issue should be there.
